question_text: Two children sit on a seesaw. Child A (30 kg) sits 2 m from the pivot. Child B (25 kg) sits 2.4 m from the pivot on the opposite side. Calculate the moment for each child (g = 10 N/kg) and determine if the seesaw will balance.
correct_answer: Child A moment = 600 N·m, Child B moment = 600 N·m, seesaw will balance
explanation: Child A: Moment = Weight × Distance = (30 × 10) × 2 = 600 N·m. Child B: Moment = (25 × 10) × 2.4 = 600 N·m. Since the moments are equal, the seesaw is balanced. For equilibrium, clockwise and counterclockwise moments must be equal.
